contents.name: zorkmid coin
contents.description: A large gold zorkmid coin. One side shows a portrait of Lord Dimwit Flathead; the other depicts the great underground dam.

contents.name: purple book
contents.description: This is an old book bound in purple leather. The title reads "The History of the Great Underground Empire".

contents.name: stamp
contents.description: This is a rare stamp depicting Lord Dimwit Flathead. It is quite valuable to collectors.

contents.name: crown
contents.description: The excessively gaudy crown of Lord Dimwit Flathead. It is encrusted with diamonds, rubies, and other precious gems, all in questionable taste.

contents.name: dead torch
contents.description: A burned out ivory torch.

sourceRoom.name: East-West Passage
sourceRoom.description: This is a narrow east-west passageway. There is a narrow stairway leading down at the north end of the room.

destinationRoom.name: Troll Room
destinationRoom.description: This is a small room with passages off in all directions. Bloodstains and deep scratches (perhaps made by an axe) mar the walls.

room.name: Troll Room

destinationRoom.name: Cellar

sourceRoom.name: Cellar
sourceRoom.description: This is a dark and damp cellar with a narrow passageway leading east, and a crawlway to the south. To the west is the bottom of a steep metal ramp which is unclimbable.

destinationRoom.name: Living Room
destinationRoom.description: This is the living room. There is a door to the east. To the west is a wooden door with strange gothic lettering, which appears to be nailed shut.

room.name: Living Room
room.description: This is the living room. There is a door to the east. To the west is a wooden door with strange gothic lettering, which appears to be nailed shut.

visibleItems.name: trophy case
visibleItems.description: A handsome trophy case with a glass front.

contents.name: portrait
contents.description: A magnificent oil portrait of J. Pierpont Flathead, founder of the Bank of Zork. The ornate gilded frame alone must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: painting
contents.description: A painting of remarkable beauty, depicting a woodland scene. It is the only painting remaining in the gallery.

contents.name: stack of zorkmid bills
contents.description: A thick stack of crisp zorkmid bills in various denominations. The bills bear the stern likeness of Lord Dimwit Flathead.

contents.name: platinum bar
contents.description: This is a large bar of platinum, stamped "FROBOZZ MAGIC COMPANY".

contents.name: Don Woods stamp
contents.description: A small commemorative postage stamp depicting a spelunker.

contents.name: gold coffin
contents.description: The solid gold coffin used for the burial of Ramses II is here.

contents.name: large emerald
contents.description: A large emerald of exceptional clarity and color.

contents.name: beautiful statue
contents.description: A beautiful statue of an ancient adventurer, carved from a single piece of white marble. The craftsmanship is exquisite.

contents.name: pot of gold
contents.description: A small iron pot filled to the brim with gold coins. It is surprisingly heavy for its size.

contents.name: pearl necklace
contents.description: A pearl necklace with hundreds of large pearls. It must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: tin of spices
contents.description: A tin of rare spices. The aroma is exotic and enticing.

contents.name: white crystal sphere
contents.description: A perfectly smooth sphere of white crystal. It glows with an inner light.

contents.name: huge diamond
contents.description: This is an enormous diamond, perfectly cut and dazzlingly brilliant. It must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: sapphire bracelet
contents.description: A delicate bracelet set with brilliant blue sapphires.

contents.name: violin
contents.description: A Stradivarius! This exquisite instrument is in perfect condition despite its surroundings. It must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: grail
contents.description: A plain wooden grail, yet it radiates an aura of ancient holiness. Its simple appearance belies its true value.

contents.name: crystal trident
contents.description: On the shore lies Poseidon's own crystal trident.

contents.name: leather bag of coins
contents.description: A leather bag containing a quantity of coins of various denominations.

contents.name: blue crystal sphere
contents.description: A beautiful sphere of blue crystal. It seems to glow with an inner light.

contents.name: jade figurine
contents.description: A beautiful jade figurine of an oriental dragon. It is exquisitely carved.

contents.name: red crystal sphere
contents.description: A beautiful sphere of red crystal. It seems to glow with an inner light.

contents.name: golden clockwork canary
contents.description: A beautiful golden clockwork canary. It sings when wound.

contents.name: brass bauble
contents.description: A shiny brass bauble, dropped by an unseen songbird in response to the canary's tune.

contents.name: silver chalice
contents.description: A beautiful silver chalice, tarnished with age but still valuable. It bears an inscription in an ancient language.

contents.name: jewel-encrusted egg
contents.description: A beautiful jewel-encrusted egg. It appears to open somehow.

contents.name: gold card
contents.description: This is an ornate gold card, beautifully embossed with the royal crest of the Great Underground Empire.

visibleItems.name: large oriental rug
visibleItems.description: A large oriental rug in the center of the room.

visibleItems.name: wooden door
visibleItems.description: A wooden door with strange gothic lettering. It appears to be nailed shut.

visibleItems.name: trap door
visibleItems.description: The trap door is open, revealing a rickety staircase descending into darkness.

attributes.name: trap door
attributes.openDescription: The trap door is open, revealing a rickety staircase descending into darkness.
attributes.closedDescription: The dusty cover of a closed trap door.

visibleItems.name: incense
visibleItems.description: A stick of ancient incense, still fragrant after all these years. It was clutched in the skeleton's bony fingers.

visibleItems.name: skeleton key
visibleItems.description: An old skeleton key, probably dropped by the unfortunate adventurer whose remains lie nearby.

visibleItems.name: large brochure
visibleItems.description: A large, glossy brochure from MIT Tech. Affixed loosely to it is a small stamp.

visibleItems.name: wrench
visibleItems.description: This is a heavy wrench, suitable for loosening large bolts.

visibleItems.name: trunk
visibleItems.description: This is an old trunk, covered in mud. It appears to contain a fortune in jewels!

visibleItems.name: brass bell
visibleItems.description: A brass bell with strange symbols engraved around its rim.

visibleItems.name: black book
visibleItems.description: An ancient book bound in black leather. Strange symbols cover the cover.

visibleItems.name: burned-out candles
visibleItems.description: A pair of burned-out candle stubs.

visibleItems.name: sharp stick
visibleItems.description: A sharp stick, which appears to have been broken at one end, is here.

visibleItems.name: blue cake
visibleItems.description: A cake with blue icing. You can make out a capital E on the icing.

visibleItems.name: eat-me cake
visibleItems.description: A cake with "Eat Me" written on it in icing. You can make out a capital E on the icing.

visibleItems.name: elongated brown sack
visibleItems.description: A brown sack smelling of hot peppers.

contents.name: lunch
contents.description: A hearty lunch.

contents.name: clove of garlic
contents.description: A clove of garlic. Its strong smell might ward off something...

visibleItems.name: frame piece
visibleItems.description: A piece of the broken frame bearing a carved inscription: "Only devotion can reveal my location."

visibleItems.description: A perfectly smooth sphere of white crystal. It glows with an inner light.

visibleItems.name: large emerald
visibleItems.description: A large emerald of exceptional clarity and color.

visibleItems.description: A beautiful statue of an ancient adventurer, carved from a single piece of white marble. The craftsmanship is exquisite.

visibleItems.description: A leather bag containing a quantity of coins of various denominations.

visibleItems.description: An old skeleton key, probably dropped by the unfortunate adventurer whose remains lie nearby.

visibleItems.name: incense
visibleItems.description: A stick of ancient incense, still fragrant after all these years. It was clutched in the skeleton's bony fingers.

visibleItems.name: silver chalice
visibleItems.description: A beautiful silver chalice, tarnished with age but still valuable. It bears an inscription in an ancient language.

visibleItems.name: violin
visibleItems.description: A Stradivarius! This exquisite instrument is in perfect condition despite its surroundings. It must be worth a fortune.

visibleItems.description: A large, glossy brochure from MIT Tech. Affixed loosely to it is a small stamp.

visibleItems.name: Don Woods stamp
visibleItems.description: A small commemorative postage stamp depicting a spelunker.

visibleItems.name: huge diamond
visibleItems.description: This is an enormous diamond, perfectly cut and dazzlingly brilliant. It must be worth a fortune.

visibleItems.name: frame piece
visibleItems.description: A piece of the broken frame bearing a carved inscription: "Only devotion can reveal my location."

visibleItems.name: brass bauble
visibleItems.description: A shiny brass bauble, dropped by an unseen songbird in response to the canary's tune.

location.name: Gallery
location.description: This is an art gallery. Most of the paintings have been stolen by vandals with exceptional taste. The vandals left through either the north or west exits.

itemSnapshot.name: rolled up canvas
itemSnapshot.description: The thief apparently had a superior artistic streak, for this is one of the greatest creations in all of Zork. It is a faithful rendering of The Implementors, the mythical Gods remembered by all inhabitants.

targetSnapshot.name: trophy case
targetSnapshot.description: A handsome trophy case with a glass front.

contents.name: portrait
contents.description: A magnificent oil portrait of J. Pierpont Flathead, founder of the Bank of Zork. The ornate gilded frame alone must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: painting
contents.description: A painting of remarkable beauty, depicting a woodland scene. It is the only painting remaining in the gallery.

contents.name: stack of zorkmid bills
contents.description: A thick stack of crisp zorkmid bills in various denominations. The bills bear the stern likeness of Lord Dimwit Flathead.

contents.name: platinum bar
contents.description: This is a large bar of platinum, stamped "FROBOZZ MAGIC COMPANY".

contents.name: Don Woods stamp
contents.description: A small commemorative postage stamp depicting a spelunker.

contents.name: gold coffin
contents.description: The solid gold coffin used for the burial of Ramses II is here.

contents.name: large emerald
contents.description: A large emerald of exceptional clarity and color.

contents.name: beautiful statue
contents.description: A beautiful statue of an ancient adventurer, carved from a single piece of white marble. The craftsmanship is exquisite.

contents.name: pot of gold
contents.description: A small iron pot filled to the brim with gold coins. It is surprisingly heavy for its size.

contents.name: pearl necklace
contents.description: A pearl necklace with hundreds of large pearls. It must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: tin of spices
contents.description: A tin of rare spices. The aroma is exotic and enticing.

contents.name: white crystal sphere
contents.description: A perfectly smooth sphere of white crystal. It glows with an inner light.

contents.name: huge diamond
contents.description: This is an enormous diamond, perfectly cut and dazzlingly brilliant. It must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: sapphire bracelet
contents.description: A delicate bracelet set with brilliant blue sapphires.

contents.name: violin
contents.description: A Stradivarius! This exquisite instrument is in perfect condition despite its surroundings. It must be worth a fortune.

contents.name: grail
contents.description: A plain wooden grail, yet it radiates an aura of ancient holiness. Its simple appearance belies its true value.

contents.name: crystal trident
contents.description: On the shore lies Poseidon's own crystal trident.

contents.name: leather bag of coins
contents.description: A leather bag containing a quantity of coins of various denominations.

contents.name: blue crystal sphere
contents.description: A beautiful sphere of blue crystal. It seems to glow with an inner light.

contents.name: jade figurine
contents.description: A beautiful jade figurine of an oriental dragon. It is exquisitely carved.

contents.name: red crystal sphere
contents.description: A beautiful sphere of red crystal. It seems to glow with an inner light.

contents.name: golden clockwork canary
contents.description: A beautiful golden clockwork canary. It sings when wound.

contents.name: brass bauble
contents.description: A shiny brass bauble, dropped by an unseen songbird in response to the canary's tune.

contents.name: silver chalice
contents.description: A beautiful silver chalice, tarnished with age but still valuable. It bears an inscription in an ancient language.

contents.name: jewel-encrusted egg
contents.description: A beautiful jewel-encrusted egg. It appears to open somehow.

contents.name: gold card
contents.description: This is an ornate gold card, beautifully embossed with the royal crest of the Great Underground Empire.

contents.name: ruby
contents.description: This is an enormous ruby, the size of a robin's egg. It sparkles brilliantly in the light.

contents.name: zorkmid coin
contents.description: A large gold zorkmid coin. One side shows a portrait of Lord Dimwit Flathead; the other depicts the great underground dam.

contents.name: stamp
contents.description: This is a rare stamp depicting Lord Dimwit Flathead. It is quite valuable to collectors.

contents.name: crown
contents.description: The excessively gaudy crown of Lord Dimwit Flathead. It is encrusted with diamonds, rubies, and other precious gems, all in questionable taste.

contents.name: trunk
contents.description: This is an old trunk, covered in mud. It appears to contain a fortune in jewels!

contents.name: dead torch
contents.description: A burned out ivory torch.

contents.name: rolled up canvas
contents.description: The thief apparently had a superior artistic streak, for this is one of the greatest creations in all of Zork. It is a faithful rendering of The Implementors, the mythical Gods remembered by all inhabitants.

To the south is a small opening, apparently of recent origin.

destinationRoom.name: Hallway
destinationRoom.description:
This is part of the long hallway. The east and west walls are dressed stone. In the center of the hall is a shallow stone channel. In the center of the room the channel widens into a large hole around which is engraved a compass rose. Sitting in the center of the room is a strange wooden structure.

Identical stone statues face each other from pedestals on opposite sides of the corridor. The statues represent Guardians of Zork, a military order of ancient lineage. They are portrayed as heavily armored warriors standing at ease, hands clasped around formidable bludgeons.

destinationRoom.name: Inside Mirror
destinationRoom.description:
You are inside a rectangular box of wood whose structure is rather complicated. Four sides and the roof are filled in, and the floor is open.

As you face the side opposite the entrance, two short sides of carved and polished wood are to your left and right. The left panel is mahogany, the right pine. The wall you face is red on its left half and black on its right. On the entrance side, the wall is white opposite the red part of the wall it faces, and yellow opposite the black section. The painted walls are at least twice the length of the unpainted ones. The ceiling is painted blue.

In the floor is a stone channel about six inches wide and a foot deep. The channel is oriented in a north-south direction. In the exact center of the room the channel widens into a circular depression perhaps two feet wide. Incised in the stone around this area is a compass rose.

Running from one short wall to the other at about waist height is a wooden bar, carefully carved and drilled. This bar is pierced in two places. Through each hole runs a wooden pole.

visibleItems.name: short pole
visibleItems.description: The pole at the left end of the bar is short, extending about a foot above the bar, and ends in a hand grip.

visibleItems.name: long pole
visibleItems.description: The long pole at the center of the bar extends from the ceiling through the bar to the circular area in the stone channel. The bottom end of this pole has a T-bar a bit less than two feet long attached to it. On the T-bar is carved an arrow.

visibleItems.name: mahogany panel
visibleItems.description: The mahogany panel is smooth and well-polished. It forms one of the short walls of the structure.

visibleItems.name: pine panel
visibleItems.description: The pine panel is sturdy but plain. It forms one of the short walls of the structure.

visibleItems.name: red panel
visibleItems.description: The red panel is painted a deep crimson. It forms part of the long wall opposite the entrance.

visibleItems.name: yellow panel
visibleItems.description: The yellow panel is painted a bright gold color. It forms part of the wall on the entrance side.

destinationRoom.name: East-West Corridor
destinationRoom.description: This is a large east-west corridor which opens out to a northern parapet at its center. You can see flames and smoke as you peer towards the parapet. The corridor turns south at its east and west ends, and due south is a massive wooden door. In the door is a small window barred with iron.

destinationRoom.name: Parapet
destinationRoom.description:
You are standing behind a stone retaining wall which rims a large parapet overlooking a fiery pit. It is difficult to see through the smoke and flame which fills the pit, but it seems to be more or less bottomless. It also extends upwards out of sight. The pit itself is of roughly dressed stone and is circular in shape. It is about two hundred feet in diameter. The flames generate considerable heat, so it is rather uncomfortable standing here.

There is an object here which looks like a sundial. On it are an indicator arrow and (in the center) a large button. On the face of the dial are numbers "one" through "eight".
